title: Add Preflight Review derivation

sources.reason: Defines the Cockpit workflow, status vocabulary, and generated artifacts.
Defines the generic governance terms and Scenario Coverage boundary.
Registers Cockpit checks and Make targets.
Shows how guard-driven policy gates are configured without hard-coding product rules.
Existing evidence-compression model to keep the new derivation consistent.
Existing contract-evidence reporting pattern to preserve generic contract readability.
Existing report-only guard pattern for policy-driven evidence checks.
Template entrypoint that must expose the new Make targets.

riskAssessment.reason: The new review must stay advisory-first, generic, and backward-compatible while deriving readiness from existing Contract evidence.

executionDecision.reason: Scope, sources, acceptance, and verification are explicit for implementing Preflight Review.

preReviewWarnings: Keep Preflight Review advisory-first by default and only fail through policy-enabled gate statuses.
Derive each signal from Contract evidence; do not add new Contract fields or self-declared readiness.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record a checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make generate-ai-preflight-review emits a readable readiness assessment for the active Work Item and writes a structured report artifact.
make check-ai-preflight-review validates the generated report structure and policy configuration while remaining advisory unless configuration enables gating.
Preflight Review derives ready, needs_human_confirmation, and not_ready from existing Contract evidence without adding new Contract fields or requiring AI self-declaration.
The assessment explains why each signal was assigned by citing the underlying Contract evidence.
The existing AI checks and current status generation continue to pass with the new Preflight Review targets and policy file in place.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Generate a Preflight Review from the active Contract
Validate the generated Preflight Review in advisory mode
Fail the check only when policy enables a blocked status

guidelines: Keep the derivation generic and policy-driven.
Do not add new Contract fields or change Summary structure.
Keep advisory behavior as the default unless a policy explicitly enables gating.

intent.problem: Add a Preflight Review that derives implementation readiness from existing Work Item Contract evidence before implementation starts.
constraints: Do not add new Contract fields or modify Summary structure.
Do not let the AI self-declare ready or not ready.
Keep the derivation generic and policy-driven.
Default to advisory behavior; only enable gating through configuration.
intent.rationale: A derived readiness view makes the pre-implementation decision explainable, generic, and auditable without introducing a new self-assessment step.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly requested changes to AI Cockpit governance files and Makefile entrypoints.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
